long_description:
Internal switch is04.sr01.sao04 has gone down.

As of 21:45 UTC 30 November 2025, Network Specialists are investigating an internal switch that services provision servers in SAO04 Datacenter. Customer may experience delays in provisions while Networking and Datacenter staff work on restoring the internal switch. 

As of 00:38 UTC 01 December 2025, Network Specialists worked with Datacenter staff on replacing the internal switch and recovered provision server access to SAO04. 

https://github.ibm.com/IaaS-NRE/nreincidents/issues/19665

No EMS - Services teams will provide communication to customers for any stalled txns.
close_notes: NRE has worked with datacenter staff to replace the faulty hardware with spare hardware. An RMA case has been initiated to replenish the failed hardware.
current_status_next_steps:
The device is in a failed state, NRE is working with Datacenter staff on replacing with spare hardware via emergency change :
CHG14132095
customer_facing_impact: As of 21:45 UTC, 30 November 2025, Network Specialists are investigating an internal switch that services provision servers in SAO04 Datacenter. Customer may experience delays in provisions while Networking and Datacenter staff work on restoring the internal switch.
comment_list:
[{"comment": "Issue Summary: is04.sr01.sao04 switch down SME Findings: NRE: SME Findings As of 21:45 30 November 2025, Network Specialists are investigating an internal switch that services provision servers in SAO04 Datacenter. Customer may experience delays in provisions while Networking and Datacenter staff work on restoring the internal switch. Reason of Escalation: NRE notified internal teams and IPOPS of this device down, those teams need to verify and report back the impact to NRE. Resolution Steps: First try power pull via CHG14132014 (did not restore switch), next replace the device via CHG14132095 Signature of Issue: Hardware Failure Jira/Github/Other Tickets: https://github.ibm.com/IaaS-NRE/nreincidents/issues/19665 Category/Subcategory: Sysems / Chassis Failure Timeline Summary: 2025-11-30 21:45:00 - Device goes down, NRE received alarms in Oculus 2025-11-30 21:57:00 NRE notified DC via slack #sl-dcops-sao04 2025-11-30 22:00:00 - Sev2 DC ticket https://internal.softlayer.com/Ticket/ticketPreview/171161190 2025-11-30 21:57:00 - INC10443177 created as normal sev4 2025-11-30 22:12:00 - INC10443177 upgraded to CIE sev4 due to provision impact seen by ipops 2025-11-30 22:16:00 - DC staff on their way to the server room 2025-11-30 22:25:00 - DC staff reports LEDs are all RED status 2025-11-30 22:26:00 - NRE asked DC to power pull 2025-11-30 22:33:00 - Arista case 755130 for RMA 2025-11-30 22:40:00 - CHG14132014 to power pull the device 2025-11-30 22:48:00 - DC completed power pull and reported same status on LED 2025-11-30 22:52:00 - NRE Lead Rey identified a spare switch and will work on replacing 2025-11-30 22:56:00 -CHG14132095 for the switch replacement 2025-11-30 23:18:00 - NRE Lead Rey completd the firmware upgrade and switch config of the spare device 2025-11-30 23:26:00 - DC staff will work on this but may take time. 2025-11-30 23:48:00 - DC staff updated that they completed labeling cables and will start the replacement now 2025-12-01 00:22:00 - DC staff update that replacement switch is now racked up and are connecting cables to it 2025-12-01 00:38:44 - DC staff completd the connection and NRE enabled ports to replacement device. Services teams confirmed no issues and they can access the provision servers. NRE will work with Arista to RMA the faulty hardware.
